title: Xtra Buttery American Lamb Flatbread
description: A buttery and molten American dish featuring lamb shanks, long-fermentation-dough, and kosher-friendly ingredients, served on a hot stone plate.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large mixing bowl, combine flour, salt, sugar, and yeast.
Gradually add warm water to the dry ingredients and mix until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Place the dough in a greased bowl, cover it with a damp cloth, and let it rise in a warm place for 1 hour.
Punch down the dough and roll it out into a large rectangle.
Place the lamb shanks in the center of the dough, leaving a 1 inch border around them.
Brush the melted butter over the lamb and dough, and sprinkle with garlic powder.
Fold the dough over the lamb, pressing the edges to seal.
Place the flatbread on a hot stone plate and bake for 25 minutes.
